# Manage artifact tags

This topic describes how to add and manage tags for items hosted in your private registry, such as modules, providers, and Stacks component configurations. 

## Overview

You can assign project tags to modules, providers, and Stack components in the registry. This helps you consistently use metadata across entities in your system. Use tags to indicate project association, describe intended environments, and apply other meaningful labels. When you tag an artifact with a project tag, they also appear on the [project overview page](/terraform/cloud-docs/projects/manage). 

<!-- 

Controlling registry artifact usage is in private beta 
Uncomment when this functionality is available as public beta or GA

You can also configure HCP Terraform to control registry artifact usage based on tags. When this functionality is enabled, Terraform verifies that the tags on the requested module or provider match the parent project tags at runtime. When the tags match, the run continues successfully. Otherwise HCP Terraform cancels the run and prints a message stating that it cannot find a matching tag.

To control access to specific module or provider versions, attach a reserved tag that has been created with the **Registry environment tag** option enabled. Refer to [Control access to registry artifacts](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/organizations/manage-reserved-tags#control-access-to-registry-artifacts) for more information.
-->

## Requirements

<!-- Controlling registry artifact usage is in private beta 
Uncomment when this functionality is available as public beta or GA

You must be on an IBM HCP Terraform premium plan to restrict access to artifact versions. Refer to the [pricing page](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/pricing?tab=terraform) for more information.

-->
You must be on an IBM HCP Terraform standard plan to add environment tags to individual artifact versions. Refer to the [pricing page](https://www.hashicorp.com/en/pricing?tab=terraform) for more information.

You can only assign existing tags to registry artifacts. Refer to [Define project tags](/terraform/cloud-docs/projects/manage#define-project-tags) for instructions on creating project tags that you can assign to items in your registry. For instructions on how to create registry environment tags, refer to [Create and manage reserved tag keys](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/organizations/manage-reserved-tags). 

The following settings must be enabled for your organization to attach tags to artifacts in the registry:

- [**Assign project tags to modules, providers, and Stack component configurations**](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/permissions/organization#assign-project-tags-to-modules-providers-and-stack-component-configurations)
- [**Assign reserved keys to module, provider, and Stack component configuration versions**](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/permissions/organization#assign-reserved-keys-to-module-provider-and-stack-component-configuration-versions)

## Attach project tags to a single registry artifact

1. Sign in to [HCP Terraform](https://app.terraform.io/) and navigate to your organization.
1. Click on **Registry** in the sidebar, then click either **Modules**, **Providers**, or **Stack component configurations** .
1. Click on the artifact, then click **Tags** in the sidebar. 
1. On the **Manage tags** page, browse for tags that you want to attach to the artifact. You can click on the link in either the **Projects** or **Workspaces** column view the projects and workspaces that have the tag. 
1. Select the tags you want to attach to the artifact, the click **Save changes**.

<!-- Not in the public beta, expose for GA or for public beta 
HCP Terraform attaches the selected tags to the artifact so that only projects that have the same tags can consume them. 
-->
## Attach project tags to registry artifacts in bulk

1. Log into HCP Terraform and navigate to your organization.
1. Click on **Registry** in the sidebar, then click either **Modules**, **Providers**, or **Stack component configurations**.
1. Click the bulk selection button for the artifact type. For example, if you clicked **Modules** in the previous step, click **Bulk select modules**. 
1. Choose the artifacts in the results table that you want to attach the same tag to.
1. Click **Assign tags** from the **Actions** drop-down.
1. When prompted, choose the tags to assign, then click **Save**.

<!-- Not in the public beta, expose for GA or for public beta 
HCP Terraform attaches the selected tags to the artifact so that only projects that have the same tags can consume them. 
-->

## Attach registry environment tags to artifact versions

When you create reserved tags for the organization, you can enable the **Registry environment tag** option, which lets you assign the tag to a module, provider, or Stack component configuration version in the registry. Refer to [Define a reserved tag key](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/organizations/manage-reserved-tags#define-a-reserved-tag-key) for information about designating an organization key as the registry environment tag. 
<!-- controlling access to reg artifacts is in private beta, uncomment when available GA or public beta 

Refer to [Control access to registry artifacts](/terraform/cloud-docs/users-teams-organizations/organizations/manage-reserved-tags#control-access-to-registry-artifacts) for more information. -->
 
1. Sign in to [HCP Terraform](https://app.terraform.io/) and navigate to your organization.
1. Click on **Registry** in the sidebar, then click either **Modules**, **Providers**, or **Stack component configuration**.
1. Click on the artifact to open its detail page.
1. Mouse over **Environment tags** link, then click **Manage environment tags** from the tooltip.
1. When prompted, choose the artifact version to assign the tags to from the drop-down.
1. Choose one or more environment tags, then click **Save**.

HCP Terraform attaches the selected environment tags to the artifact version. 
<!-- Not in the public beta, expose for GA or for public beta 
As a result, only projects that have the same tags can consume that version of the artifact. 
-->

The tags are also available in the **Filters** section in the registry so that you can more easily browse for suitable artifacts to use for your workspaces. Refer to [Use artifacts from the HCP Terraform private registry](/terraform/cloud-docs/registry/using#find-artifacts) for more information.
